title: No Power to Rh Wire
shortDescription: The thermostat is not receiving 24V power from the heating system's Rh wire.
fullDescription: Nest error E73 indicates the thermostat is not detecting power on the Rh (heating power) wire. The Nest needs at least one power wire (Rc or Rh) to operate. Without it, the Nest will drain its battery and shut down.

causes.cause: Loose Rh wire at thermostat base
Blown fuse on HVAC control board

fixSteps.title: Check Wire
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the Nest display and verify the Rh wire is fully inserted into the Rh terminal on the base.

fixSteps.title: Check Breaker
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the furnace/boiler breaker is on at the electrical panel.

fixSteps.title: Check Fuse
fixSteps.instruction: At the HVAC control board, check the 3A or 5A fuse.

fixSteps.title: Test Voltage
fixSteps.instruction: Measure for 24VAC between Rh and C at the HVAC control board.

whenToCallPro: If the fuse blows again after replacement — indicates a wiring short.

faq.q: Can I use Rc instead of Rh?
faq.a: If you have a single transformer system, you can jumper Rc to Rh with the Nest's built-in jumper.

faq.q: Why does my Nest keep dying?
faq.a: Without proper power wiring, the Nest relies on battery alone which drains within hours.

faq.q: What fuse do I need?
faq.a: Typically a 3A automotive-style fuse — check your HVAC board.

title: No Power to Rc Wire
shortDescription: The thermostat is not receiving 24V power from the cooling system's Rc wire.
fullDescription: Error E74 means the Nest is not detecting power on the Rc (cooling power) wire. If your system uses separate transformers for heating and cooling, both must supply power for full functionality.

fixSteps.title: Check Wire
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the Rc wire is fully inserted in the base terminal.

fixSteps.title: Check Breaker
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the AC/air handler breaker is on.

fixSteps.title: Check Fuse
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the fuse on the air handler control board.

fixSteps.title: Test Voltage
fixSteps.instruction: Measure 24VAC between Rc and C at the air handler.

whenToCallPro: If voltage is absent with a good fuse — transformer or wiring issue.

faq.q: Do I need both Rh and Rc?
faq.a: Only if you have separate heating and cooling transformers. Most systems use a single transformer.

faq.q: Can I jumper Rh and Rc?
faq.a: Yes, the Nest has a built-in jumper for single-transformer systems.

faq.q: Is E74 an emergency?
faq.a: No, but cooling will not work until it's resolved.

title: No C Wire Detected
shortDescription: The common (C) wire is not connected or not providing power.
fullDescription: Error N72 indicates the Nest thermostat does not detect a C (common) wire. While the Nest can operate without one by pulse-charging through the HVAC system, this can cause issues with some equipment and lead to battery drain.

causes.cause: No C wire installed in home
C wire disconnected at HVAC unit

fixSteps.title: Check for C Wire
fixSteps.instruction: Look behind the old thermostat base or in the wall — there may be an unused blue wire.

fixSteps.title: Connect C Wire
fixSteps.instruction: If a blue wire exists, connect it to C terminal on the Nest and at the HVAC board.

fixSteps.title: Use Adapter
fixSteps.instruction: Install the Nest Power Connector or a C-wire adapter kit.

fixSteps.title: Run New Wire
fixSteps.instruction: If no spare wire exists, run a new thermostat cable.

whenToCallPro: If no spare wire exists and running new cable is needed.

faq.q: Can Nest work without a C wire?
faq.a: Yes, but it may cause issues. Installing a C wire or adapter is strongly recommended.

faq.q: What is the Nest Power Connector?
faq.a: An adapter that installs at the HVAC board to provide proper C wire power to the Nest.

faq.q: Why does my Nest keep losing WiFi?
faq.a: Low battery from no C wire causes the Nest to disable WiFi to conserve power.

title: Heat Pump Balance Issue
shortDescription: The heat pump is running auxiliary heat more than expected.
fullDescription: Warning W5 on a Nest thermostat indicates the system is relying on auxiliary electric heat more than the heat pump alone. This significantly increases energy costs.

causes.cause: Heat pump balance point set too high

symptoms: High electric bills in heating season
Heat pump outdoor unit not running

fixSteps.title: Check Nest Setup
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the system type is correctly configured as a heat pump in Nest settings.

fixSteps.title: Adjust Balance
fixSteps.instruction: Set the heat pump balance in Nest to a lower temperature to reduce auxiliary heat use.

fixSteps.title: Check Outdoor Unit
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the heat pump outdoor unit runs when heating is called.

fixSteps.title: Professional Tune-Up
fixSteps.instruction: Schedule a heat pump inspection including refrigerant charge check.

whenToCallPro: If the outdoor heat pump unit is not running during heating calls.

faq.q: What is heat pump balance?
faq.a: The outdoor temperature below which auxiliary electric heat supplements the heat pump.

faq.q: How much more does aux heat cost?
faq.a: Auxiliary electric heat costs 2-3 times more than heat pump operation.

faq.q: What should the balance point be?
faq.a: Typically 30-35°F, but it depends on your system and climate.

title: Nest Display Disconnected
shortDescription: The Nest display unit has lost connection with its base plate.
fullDescription: Error E298 indicates the Nest thermostat display is not properly connected to the wall base. This can be caused by improper seating, dirt on the connectors, or a damaged base plate.

causes.cause: Display not fully seated on base

fixSteps.title: Reseat Display
fixSteps.instruction: Pull the Nest display off the base and firmly push it back on until it clicks.

fixSteps.title: Clean Connectors
fixSteps.instruction: Gently clean the connector pins on both the display and base with a dry soft cloth.

fixSteps.title: Check Base Level
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the base plate is level and flush against the wall.

fixSteps.title: Inspect Base Pins
fixSteps.instruction: Look for bent or broken connector pins on the base plate.

whenToCallPro: If pins are damaged on the base plate — a replacement base may be needed.

faq.q: How do I remove the Nest display?
faq.a: Gently pull it straight out from the base — it is held by magnets and connector pins.

faq.q: Can I buy just the base plate?
faq.a: Yes, replacement base plates are available from Google's Nest store.

faq.q: Why does my Nest keep disconnecting?
faq.a: A loose display-to-base connection is the most common cause.

title: Delayed Cooling/Heating
shortDescription: The Nest has delayed starting cooling or heating to protect the HVAC equipment.
fullDescription: Warning W8 appears when the Nest thermostat intentionally delays activating the HVAC system. This protective delay prevents compressor short cycling, which can cause damage. The delay is typically 2-5 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Wait
fixSteps.instruction: This is normal — wait 2-5 minutes for the protection delay to expire.

fixSteps.title: Avoid Rapid Changes
fixSteps.instruction: Avoid repeatedly changing the temperature or switching modes quickly.

fixSteps.title: Check After Delay
fixSteps.instruction: If the system does not start after 10 minutes, investigate further.

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: If stuck in delay, remove the Nest display for 10 seconds and replace it.

whenToCallPro: Only if the delay never clears and the system does not start after 10 minutes.

faq.q: Why does the Nest delay the system?
faq.a: To protect the compressor from short cycling, which can cause premature failure.

faq.q: How long is the delay?
faq.a: Typically 2-5 minutes. It should resolve on its own.

faq.q: Can I disable the delay?
faq.a: No — it is a built-in safety feature that cannot be turned off.

title: Software Update Failed
shortDescription: A Nest software update failed to install properly.
fullDescription: Error E195 indicates a Nest firmware update did not complete successfully. The thermostat may be running an outdated or partially updated firmware version, which can cause operational issues.

fixSteps.title: Check WiFi
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the Nest is connected to WiFi with a strong signal.

fixSteps.title: Check Battery
fixSteps.instruction: In Nest settings, verify the battery voltage is above 3.6V.

fixSteps.title: Restart Nest
fixSteps.instruction: Pull the display off the base for 10 seconds, then reattach to trigger a restart.

fixSteps.title: Force Update
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Software > Update to manually trigger a firmware update.

whenToCallPro: Only if repeated update failures occur — contact Google Nest support.

faq.q: Can I roll back a Nest update?
faq.a: No, Nest updates cannot be manually rolled back.

faq.q: Are updates automatic?
faq.a: Yes, Nest updates firmware automatically over WiFi.

faq.q: Will a failed update brick my Nest?
faq.a: Very rarely. Most update failures can be resolved with a restart and retry.

title: No Heating Detected
shortDescription: The Nest called for heat but no temperature rise was detected.
fullDescription: Error H71 means the Nest thermostat has been calling for heating for an extended period but the temperature sensor has not detected the expected temperature increase. This suggests the heating system is not functioning.
symptoms: Furnace not running or running without heat

fixSteps.title: Check Furnace
fixSteps.instruction: Go to the furnace and check if it is running. Look for LED error codes on the control board.

fixSteps.title: Check Filter
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the air filter if dirty.

fixSteps.title: Verify Wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Check that the W (heat) wire is properly connected at both the Nest base and the furnace.

fixSteps.title: Test Heat
fixSteps.instruction: Feel the supply vents after 5 minutes of heating call — air should be warm.

whenToCallPro: If the furnace has error codes or does not ignite.

faq.q: Why does my Nest say heating but no heat?
faq.a: The Nest is sending the signal, but the furnace is not responding — check the furnace for errors.

faq.q: Can wrong wiring cause no heat?
faq.a: Yes — if the W wire is not connected or is on the wrong terminal.

faq.q: How long before the Nest detects no heat?
faq.a: Typically 1-2 hours of no temperature rise triggers this alert.
